item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had translated 1907 into Chinese.Hope guys from CN-site could like it.XD
Seriously,I don't understand what 1907 is until I read the audio log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nobody's touched this in a while, eh?

I like how most of the violence in this is just a result of cultural shock on the part of foreign beings, and there is a slight weirdness with how this reads, can't say anything specific.

+1, because interdimensional tourism is a market ripe for monopolizing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this has much potential and I definitely like what you have so far.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Tour Guide is— or rather, was— human. That's how they capably handle telling people about that specific world (by extention, every world that has entities floating around in there has similar portals that do the same thing, and they have whatever species is already there be the tour guide for that specific place because they are the most able. And if the language barrier is a problem, consider the fact that all of them were floating around by this and able to communicate fluidly via concepts and ideas, and could easily share the knowledge of the languages. However, learning in theory and doing in practice are, of course, two entirely separate beasts).

As for the polishing up, since that seems to be a major point of criticism, I will probably work on this and have it updated within the next few days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hrmrmrmrm….

I'm no-voting on this for now, but it's close to my upvote… something about it seems not-quite polished enough. I like the core idea; strange extradimensional aliens walking around inside human bodies, causing them to act oddly due to e.g. not being used to having mouths is a very good one. I think I agree that the Tour Guide at the end is slightly too on-the-nose and undermines the creepiness of the core concept somewhat - I think it'd improve the article to rewrite that so it felt more inhuman (while still recognisably something similar to a tour guide - a tough balancing act).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, mostly. Although I agree it doesn't seem quite all there. There's just something missing, or maybe something isn't missing that should be. Hard to say, but regardless, it's enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's quite a good read, not the best but quite intricate.

As well, like I've said before:

Passing through SCP-1907 causes subjects to perceive a currently unnamed location. Within this space, subjects report a feeling of freedom and unity, as well as communication with various other unidentified life forms.

FOR 'MURICA.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one certainly has it's faults, but somehow it just got me. Being all inexplicably anomalous and creepy up until the last moment, then pulling that made me laugh.
Very rarely can something on this site be creepy but leave me laughing, this gets a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is kinda cool. One thing you ought to consider is specifying that the -A corpses are human when you initially mention them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm… not entirely sure what my feelings are about this. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If this continues to linger in the lower ranges within the next day or two, I'll probably take it down and come at it with a different approach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really digging this until the very last audio transcript. It felt wonderfully creepy and atmospheric until LOL, TOUR GUIDE. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like certain parts of this and dislike others. The clicking and weird behaviors are a cool way to show unfamiliarity with the human body, but they also feel a little too conveniently weird and violent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It feels a bit… forced. Especially the "oh you tried to eat their necks" bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For a reason that's difficult for me to describe, I found myself really disliking this. It wasn't the writing (which was just fine), or even the general idea (which I found inoffensive if not too interesting), it's some vague unpleasantness that's really putting me off this. I'm really sorry I can't be more helpful with this, but sometimes you just have to follow your gut.
